---
title: "What about oil price evolution?"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, echo = FALSE, include = FALSE, warning = FALSE,}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

if (requireNamespace("rstudioapi", quietly = TRUE) && rstudioapi::isAvailable()) {
  # Interactive in RStudio: use the current editor document path
  rmd_path <- rstudioapi::getActiveDocumentContext()$path
} else {
  # When knitting/rendering: knitr knows the input
  rmd_path <- knitr::current_input(dir = TRUE)
}
if (!is.null(rmd_path) && nzchar(rmd_path)) {
  knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = dirname(normalizePath(rmd_path)))
  setwd(dirname(normalizePath(rmd_path)))
}
```

```{r data_load}
df.eu.gas.price <- read.csv('../data/oil_prices/energy-eu-gas-price.csv')
df.eu.gas.price$date = as.Date(df.eu.gas.price$date)



nb_samples = nrow(subset(df.eu.gas.price, country == 'EU'))

df.eu.gas.price <- df.eu.gas.price %>%
  group_by(country) %>%
  filter(n() == nb_samples) %>%
  ungroup()

countries = unique(df.eu.gas.price$country)

df.brent = read.csv('../data/oil_prices/DCOILBRENTEU.csv')
df.brent$date = as.Date(df.brent$observation_date)
df.brent$usd = df.brent$DCOILBRENTEU
df.brent$DCOILBRENTEU <- NULL
df.brent$observation_date <- NULL

df.fx = read.csv('../data/oil_prices/ECB Data Portal_20260314001229.csv')
df.fx$date = as.Date(df.fx$DATE)
df.fx$DATE <- NULL
df.fx$TIME.PERIOD <- NULL
df.fx$usd.eur = df.fx$US.dollar.Euro.ECB.reference.exchange.rate..EXR.D.USD.EUR.SP00.A.
df.fx$US.dollar.Euro.ECB.reference.exchange.rate..EXR.D.USD.EUR.SP00.A. <- NULL

df.brent = merge(df.brent, df.fx)
df.brent$eur = df.brent$usd / df.brent$usd.eur


start.per.country = subset(df.eu.gas.price, date == min(df.eu.gas.price$date) & gas_type == 'euro95')
rownames(start.per.country) <- start.per.country$country

df.euro95 = df.eu.gas.price %>%
  filter(gas_type == "euro95")

## adding relative price taking the base on first date
df.euro95$rel_price = df.euro95$price_per_liter_euro_with_tax / start.per.country[df.euro95$country,]$price_per_liter_euro_with_tax


df.brent$rel_price = df.brent$eur / (subset(df.brent, date == min(df.brent$date))$eur[1])

```
```{r oil_price_france}
ggplot(
  subset(df.eu.gas.price, country == 'FR'),
  aes(
    x = date,
    y = price_per_liter_euro_with_tax,
    colour = gas_type,
    group = gas_type
  )
) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_line(
    data = df.brent,
    aes(x = date, y = rel_price),
    colour = 'black',
    lwd = 0.5,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```
```{r oil_price_euro95}
ggplot(
  subset(df.eu.gas.price, gas_type == 'euro95'),
  aes(
    x = date,
    y = price_per_liter_euro_with_tax,
    colour = country,
    group = country
  )
) +
  geom_line() +
  theme_minimal()
```
```{r oil_price_euro95_country_compare}




ggplot(
  subset(df.euro95, gas_type == "euro95"),
  aes(
    x = date,
    y = rel_price,
    colour = country,
    group = country
  )
) +
  geom_smooth(
    se = FALSE,
    span=0.1    ,
    method = 'loess'
  ) +
  geom_line(
    data = df.brent,
    aes(x = date, y = rel_price),
    colour = 'black',
    lwd = 0.5,
    alpha=0.7,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r oil_price_pairwise_correlation_heatmap}
library(tidyr)

# Build a country x date matrix for euro95 prices
df_euro95_wide <- df.eu.gas.price %>%
  filter(gas_type == "euro95") %>%
  filter(!country %in% c('EU', 'EUR')) %>%
  select(date, country, price_per_liter_euro_with_tax) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = country, values_from = price_per_liter_euro_with_tax) %>%
  arrange(date)

# Pairwise correlation matrix across countries
country_cols <- setdiff(names(df_euro95_wide), "date")
corr_matrix <- cor(df_euro95_wide[country_cols], use = "pairwise.complete.obs")

# Reorder to group similar countries visually
country_order <- colnames(corr_matrix)[hclust(as.dist(1 - corr_matrix))$order]

df_heatmap <- as.data.frame(as.table(corr_matrix)) %>%
  rename(country_x = Var1, country_y = Var2, correlation = Freq)

df_heatmap = subset(df_heatmap, country_x != country_y)

ggplot(df_heatmap, aes(
  x = factor(country_x, levels = country_order),
  y = factor(country_y, levels = rev(country_order)),
  fill = log(1 - correlation)
)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.1) +
  coord_fixed() +
  labs(
    title = "Country-to-Country Correlation Heat Map (euro95)",
    subtitle = "Correlation of price evolution over time",
    x = NULL,
    y = NULL,
    fill = "r"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    panel.grid = element_blank()
  )
```

```{r oil_price_pairwise_correlation_heatmap_clustered}
library(tidyr)
library(seriation)

# Build a country x date matrix for euro95 prices
df_euro95_wide <- df.eu.gas.price %>%
  filter(gas_type == "euro95") %>%
  filter(!country %in% c('EU', 'EUR')) %>%
  select(date, country, price_per_liter_euro_with_tax) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = country, values_from = price_per_liter_euro_with_tax) %>%
  arrange(date)

# Pairwise correlation matrix across countries
country_cols <- setdiff(names(df_euro95_wide), "date")
corr_matrix <- cor(df_euro95_wide[country_cols], use = "pairwise.complete.obs")

# Build a valid distance matrix and optimize leaf order with OLO
corr_matrix[is.na(corr_matrix)] <- 0
diag(corr_matrix) <- 1
dist_mat <- 1 - corr_matrix
dist_mat[dist_mat < 0] <- 0
dist_matrix <- as.dist(dist_mat)
olo <- seriate(dist_matrix, method = "OLO")
country_order <- labels(dist_matrix)[get_order(olo)]

# Long format for ggplot heatmap
df_heatmap <- as.data.frame(as.table(corr_matrix)) %>%
  rename(country_x = Var1, country_y = Var2, correlation = Freq) %>%
  mutate(
    country_x = factor(country_x, levels = country_order),
    country_y = factor(country_y, levels = rev(country_order))
  )

ggplot(df_heatmap, aes(
  x = country_x,
  y = country_y,
  fill = correlation
)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.1) +
  coord_fixed() +
  labs(
    title = "Clustered Correlation Heat Map (euro95, OLO)",
    subtitle = "Countries are ordered with optimal leaf ordering (seriation)",
    x = NULL,
    y = NULL,
    fill = "r"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    panel.grid = element_blank()
  )
```

```{r oil_price_correlation}
library(tidyr)
library(zoo)

# Filter for euro95 and prepare wide format
df_wide <- df.eu.gas.price %>%
  filter(gas_type == "euro95") %>%
  filter(!country %in% c('EUR')) %>%
  select(date, country, price_per_liter_euro_with_tax) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = country, values_from = price_per_liter_euro_with_tax) %>%
  arrange(date)

# We'll correlate each country with the EU average (EU column)
countries <- setdiff(names(df_wide), c("date", "EU"))
window_size <- 52 # 1 year rolling correlation

# Calculate rolling correlation for each country vs EU.
# Each window must be aligned on both series (country and EU), so we roll on row indices.
df_corr <- tibble(date = df_wide$date)
for (country in countries) {
  df_corr[[paste0("corr_", country)]] <- zoo::rollapplyr(
    seq_len(nrow(df_wide)),
    width = window_size,
    FUN = function(idx) {
      x <- df_wide[[country]][idx]
      y <- df_wide$EU[idx]
      keep <- complete.cases(x, y)
      if (sum(keep) < 2) {
        return(NA_real_)
      }
      x <- x[keep]
      y <- y[keep]
      if (sd(x) == 0 || sd(y) == 0) {
        return(NA_real_)
      }
      cor(x, y)
    },
    fill = NA
  )
}

df_corr <- df_corr %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("corr_"),
               names_to = "country",
               values_to = "correlation",
               names_prefix = "corr_") %>%
  filter(!is.na(correlation))

ggplot(df_corr, aes(x = date, y = correlation)) +
  geom_line() +
  facet_wrap(~country) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 22) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(size=13)
  ) +
    scale_x_date(date_labels = "%y") +
  labs(title = "Correlation of euro95 with EU Average",
       subtitle = "Rolling 1-year correlation of prices, by country",
       y = "Correlation Coefficient",
       x = "Date") +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```
